payload.body:
## Summary
Build the index builder that scans `~/agents/.tb/` and generates a queryable `index.json`.

## Context
The index is the foundation for all query, enable/disable, and audit operations. It needs to be fast, accurate, and include rich metadata.

## Requirements

### Create src/index-builder.ts
Core module that:
1. Scans `~/agents/.tb/skills/` for skill directories (each containing SKILL.md)
2. Scans `~/agents/.tb/mcp/servers.json` for MCP entries
3. Scans `~/agents/.tb/agents/` for agent profile files
4. Scans `~/agents/.tb/snippets/` for snippet files

### SKILL.md parsing
Extract metadata from SKILL.md:
- Description (from frontmatter `description:` or first paragraph)
- Tags (from frontmatter `tags:` if present)
- Name (from directory name)

### Index schema
Write to `~/agents/.tb/index.json`:

```typescript
interface FacultIndex {
  version: number;
  updatedAt: string;
  skills: { [name: string]: SkillEntry };
  mcp: { servers: { [name: string]: McpEntry } };
  agents: { [name: string]: AgentEntry };
  snippets: { [name: string]: SnippetEntry };
}
```

### Commands
- `facult index` — rebuild index from current state
- `facult index --force` — full reindex, clear any cached state

### CLI integration
Update `src/index.ts` to add the `index` command.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Index correctly lists all skills with descriptions
- [ ] Index correctly lists all MCP servers
- [ ] Index includes lastModified timestamps
- [ ] `facult index` completes in < 2s for typical installs
- [ ] Index file is human-readable JSON

## Effort
2 days

## Phase
Phase 3: Normalize & Index

## Dependencies
- T-001 (schema enhancements) should be complete for full provenance
payload.title: T-002: Index builder implementation

payload.body:
## Summary
Implement query engine for filtering/searching the index, plus `list` and `show` CLI commands.

## Context
Users need to quickly find and inspect skills, MCP servers, and agents. The query engine powers all filtering operations.

## Requirements

### Create src/query.ts
Query engine with:
- Filter by type: `skills`, `mcp`, `agents`, `snippets`
- Filter by enabled-for: `--enabled-for cursor`
- Filter by audit status: `--untrusted`, `--flagged`
- Filter by tags: `--tag automation`
- Full-text search in names and descriptions

### list command
```bash
facult list                    # List all (summary)
facult list skills             # List skills only
facult list mcp                # List MCP servers
facult list agents             # List agent profiles
facult list snippets           # List snippets

# Filters
facult list --enabled-for cursor
facult list --untrusted
facult list --flagged
facult list --json             # JSON output
```

Output format (human):
```
Skills (12)
  github          GitHub CLI integration          cursor, claude
  weather         Weather forecasts               cursor
  ...

MCP Servers (5)
  filesystem      File system access              cursor, claude
  ...
```

### show command
```bash
facult show github             # Show skill details
facult show mcp:filesystem     # Show MCP server (mcp: prefix)
facult show --json github      # JSON output
```

Output includes:
- Full description
- Source/provenance
- Enabled-for tools
- Trust/audit status
- File contents (SKILL.md or server config)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `list` shows all items with key metadata
- [ ] `list skills` filters correctly
- [ ] `--enabled-for` filter works
- [ ] `show` displays full details including content
- [ ] `mcp:` prefix disambiguates MCP servers
- [ ] JSON output available for scripting

## Effort
1–2 days

## Phase
Phase 3: Normalize & Index

## Dependencies
- T-002 (index builder)
payload.title: T-003: Query engine and list/show commands

payload.body:
## Summary
Implement conservative conflict resolution during consolidation with diff preview and user choice.

## Context
From Council Deliberation: "Auto-merge only when normalized content hashes match. Otherwise, interactive prompt with diff and options (keep-newest, keep-local, keep-remote, keep-both)."

This is critical for safety — we must not silently lose user customizations or merge malicious content.

## Requirements

### Content normalization
Before comparing, normalize content:
- Trim leading/trailing whitespace
- Normalize line endings to LF
- Optionally: normalize JSON formatting for config files

### Hash comparison
```typescript
function contentHash(content: string): string {
  const normalized = normalize(content);
  return Bun.hash(normalized).toString(16);
}
```

### Conflict detection
When consolidating an item that already exists:
1. Compare normalized hashes
2. If identical → auto-merge silently
3. If different → show conflict UI

### Conflict UI (clacks)
```
⚠️  Conflict: skill "github" exists with different content

  Current (consolidated):
    Path: ~/agents/.tb/skills/github/SKILL.md
    Modified: 2026-01-15 10:30
    Size: 2.4 KB

  Incoming (from cursor):
    Path: ~/.cursor/skills/github/SKILL.md
    Modified: 2026-01-28 14:20
    Size: 2.6 KB

  [v] View diff
  [1] Keep current (skip incoming)
  [2] Keep incoming (overwrite)
  [3] Keep both (rename incoming to github-cursor)
  [c] Cancel
```

### Diff preview
When user selects "View diff":
- Show unified diff
- Highlight additions/deletions
- Return to choice prompt after viewing

### --auto modes
For scripting/batch operations:
```bash
facult consolidate --auto keep-newest   # Keep most recently modified
facult consolidate --auto keep-current  # Skip all conflicts
facult consolidate --auto keep-incoming # Overwrite all
```

### Archive originals
When overwriting, archive the replaced version:
- `~/agents/.tb/skills/github/SKILL.md.bak.2026-01-29`
- Or in a separate archive directory

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Identical normalized content auto-merges silently
- [ ] Different content triggers interactive prompt
- [ ] Diff preview shows clear comparison
- [ ] All choice options work correctly
- [ ] `--auto` modes work for batch operations
- [ ] Replaced files are archived, not deleted

## Effort
2–3 days

## Phase
Phase 4: Managed Mode & Sync

## Security Note
From Council: "Avoid auto-merging semantic changes. Merging could hide malicious inserts; require explicit review for differences."
payload.title: T-004: Consolidation conflict workflow

payload.body:
## Summary
Implement "managed mode" where facult takes control of a tool's skill and MCP configuration via backup + symlink pattern.

## Context
From spec: "Per-tool opt-in to let facult manage. Backup existing, symlink to central, facult writes tool-specific configs from central state."

This is the core value prop — one source of truth, many tools consume it.

## Requirements

### manage command
```bash
facult manage cursor
```

Flow:
1. Check if tool is already managed → error if so
2. Detect tool paths (use adapter, see T-007/T-008)
3. Backup existing:
   - `~/.cursor/skills/` → `~/.cursor/skills.bak/`
   - `~/.cursor/mcp.json` → `~/.cursor/mcp.json.bak`
4. Create skills directory if needed
5. Create per-skill symlinks for enabled skills:
   - `~/.cursor/skills/github` → `~/agents/.tb/skills/github`
6. Generate tool-specific `mcp.json` from canonical `servers.json`
7. Record in `~/.facult/managed.json`

### unmanage command
```bash
facult unmanage cursor
```

Flow:
1. Check if tool is managed → error if not
2. Remove symlinks
3. Restore from backup (if exists)
4. Remove from `managed.json`

### managed command
```bash
facult managed
```

Output:
```
Managed tools:
  cursor      Skills: 8 enabled, MCP: 5 servers
  claude      Skills: 12 enabled, MCP: 5 servers
```

### managed.json schema
```json
{
  "version": 1,
  "tools": {
    "cursor": {
      "managedAt": "2026-01-29T...",
      "skillsPath": "~/.cursor/skills",
      "mcpPath": "~/.cursor/mcp.json",
      "backups": {
        "skills": "~/.cursor/skills.bak",
        "mcp": "~/.cursor/mcp.json.bak"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Per-skill symlinks
For granular enable/disable:
- Each enabled skill gets its own symlink
- Disabled skills have no symlink
- This allows per-tool skill selection

### Safety checks
- Verify backup succeeded before modifying
- Check for existing symlinks (don't double-manage)
- Validate tool paths exist
- Warn if tool is running (optional)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `manage cursor` backs up and symlinks correctly
- [ ] `unmanage cursor` restores from backup
- [ ] `managed` lists all managed tools
- [ ] Per-skill symlinks work (not directory symlink)
- [ ] Backup restoration is reliable
- [ ] Error handling for permission issues

## Effort
2–3 days

## Phase
Phase 4: Managed Mode & Sync

## Dependencies
- T-007 (adapter architecture) for tool path detection
payload.title: T-005: Managed mode implementation

payload.body:
## Summary
Implement commands to enable/disable skills and MCP servers per-tool, and sync changes to managed tools.

## Context
Once tools are in managed mode, users need fine-grained control over what's enabled where.

## Requirements

### enable command
```bash
facult enable github --for cursor,claude
facult enable mcp:filesystem --for cursor
facult enable weather  # Enable for all managed tools
```

Flow:
1. Validate item exists in index
2. Update `enabledFor` array in index.json
3. For each managed tool in `--for`:
   - Skills: create symlink if not exists
   - MCP: regenerate tool's mcp.json
4. Save updated index

### disable command
```bash
facult disable github --for cursor
facult disable mcp:filesystem --for claude
facult disable weather  # Disable for all
```

Flow:
1. Update `enabledFor` array (remove tools)
2. For each managed tool:
   - Skills: remove symlink
   - MCP: regenerate tool's mcp.json without this server
3. Save updated index

### sync command
```bash
facult sync              # Sync all managed tools
facult sync cursor       # Sync specific tool
facult sync --dry-run    # Show what would change
```

Sync ensures managed tools match current index state:
1. For each managed tool:
   - Add missing symlinks for enabled skills
   - Remove symlinks for disabled skills
   - Regenerate mcp.json from servers.json + enabledFor

### --dry-run output
```
Would sync cursor:
  + Add symlink: github
  - Remove symlink: old-skill
  ~ Regenerate mcp.json (2 servers added, 1 removed)

Would sync claude:
  (no changes)
```

### Batch operations
```bash
facult enable github weather codex --for cursor
```

Enable multiple items at once.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `enable` creates symlinks and updates index
- [ ] `disable` removes symlinks and updates index
- [ ] `sync` brings managed tools into alignment
- [ ] `--dry-run` shows changes without applying
- [ ] MCP config regenerated correctly on enable/disable
- [ ] Batch enable/disable works

## Effort
2 days

## Phase
Phase 4: Managed Mode & Sync

## Dependencies
- T-005 (managed mode)
- T-008 (adapters for MCP generation)
payload.title: T-006: Enable/disable and sync commands
T-007: Adapter architecture

payload.body:
## Summary
Implement strict validation for snippet markers to prevent injection attacks and ensure consistent behavior.

## Context
From Council Security Officer: "Ensure snippet replacement does not allow injection by validating marker names."

Snippet markers use HTML comments: `<!-- fclty:name -->...<!-- /fclty:name -->`

## Requirements

### Valid marker names
Allowed characters:
- Alphanumeric: a-z, A-Z, 0-9
- Hyphens and underscores: -, _
- Forward slashes for scoping: /

Valid examples:
- `fclty:codingstyle`
- `fclty:global/boundaries`
- `fclty:myproject/context`
- `fclty:my-snippet_v2`

### Invalid patterns to reject
- Path traversal: `fclty:../../../etc/passwd`
- Empty names: `fclty:`
- Special characters: `fclty:foo;bar`, `fclty:foo$(cmd)`
- Whitespace: `fclty:foo bar`
- Leading/trailing slashes: `fclty:/foo`, `fclty:foo/`

### Validation function
```typescript
function validateMarkerName(name: string): { valid: boolean; error?: string } {
  if (!name) return { valid: false, error: 'Empty marker name' };
  if (name.includes('..')) return { valid: false, error: 'Path traversal not allowed' };
  if (!/^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+(\/[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)*$/.test(name)) {
    return { valid: false, error: 'Invalid characters in marker name' };
  }
  return { valid: true };
}
```

### Error messaging
When invalid marker found:
```
Error: Invalid snippet marker "tb:../foo" in CLAUDE.md:15
  Path traversal (..) not allowed in marker names.
  Valid format: fclty:name or fclty:scope/name
  Examples: fclty:codingstyle, fclty:global/boundaries
```

### Integration points
1. Snippet sync command — validate before replacing
2. Snippet show/list — validate when reading
3. Create command — validate name input

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Path traversal rejected
- [ ] Special characters rejected
- [ ] Clear error messages with line numbers
- [ ] Valid scoped names work (tb:global/foo)
- [ ] Regex covers all edge cases

## Effort
1 day

## Phase
Phase 5: Snippets & Templates

## Security Note
This is a security boundary — marker names must not allow arbitrary file access or command injection.
payload.title: T-009: Snippet marker validation
